title: Number Guessing Game
description: Write a program that simulates a number guessing game. Set a variable `secret` to `7`. Set a variable `guess` to `3`. Use an **if/else** to check: if `guess` equals `secret`, print `'Correct! You guessed it!'`; otherwise print `'Wrong. The secret number was 7.'`
starter_code: # Set secret and guess, then check with if/else

code_requirements.fail_message: Create a variable called 'secret' and set it to 7.
Use an 'if' statement to compare guess to secret instead of hardcoding which message prints.

code_requirements.description: Uses the equality operator (==) to compare values
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the == operator to check if guess equals secret.

hints: Use if guess == secret: to compare them
The else branch should print the secret number using: print('Wrong. The secret number was ' + str(secret) + '.')
